title: 5 Posting Incoming AFT Files
body_text:
5	Posting Incoming AFT Files
5.1	Overview of Posting Incoming AFT Files
Incoming AFT transactions are posted to the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account by run, by net settlement, by branch, and by currency. Financial institutions process up to four Canadian dollar AFT runs per calendar day.
BC has one US AFT run per business day, and Ontario has two US AFT runs per business day. Although the files are delivered to the banking system service providers (in-house or third-party) daily, settlement to the financial institution芒聙聶s account occurs only on business days.
5.2	Serviceability Codes
CPA Rule F1, Section 8, Credit Transaction Funds Availability, specifies that direct deposits must be available to the payee as follows, based on the financial institution芒聙聶s Serviceability Code:
Serviceability Code 芒聙聹0芒聙聺 芒聙聯 AFT credit transactions must be made available to the member within two hours of receiving the AFT clearing file that contains the AFT credit transaction.
Serviceability Code 芒聙聹1芒聙聺 or 芒聙聹2芒聙聺 芒聙聯 AFT credit transactions must be made available to the member by the start of business on the due date of the transaction.
Financial institutions that use Serviceability Code 芒聙聹0芒聙聺
must ensure that incoming Canadian AFT credit transactions are posted and made available to members芒聙聶 accounts on the due date, within two hours of receiving the AFT clearing data from Central 1. If a system problem prevents automatic posting of incoming direct deposits on the due date within the required time frame, see Section 5.5, Posting Incoming AFT Transactions to Member Accounts 芒聙聯 Manually, in the subsection labelled System Problem 芒聙聯 Manually Posting Direct Deposits on Due Date.
Financial institutions must complete the Central 1 Account Request, Form 3571, to enroll in 2-hour funds availability and request a change to their serviceability code for incoming AFT transactions. Completion of this form is self-explanatory. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Central 1 Account Request, Form 3571
Select
Enroll in 2-hour funds availability
in the Request Type section to change the service to Serviceability Code 芒聙聹0芒聙聺.
Indicate that you have successfully completed internal testing on 2-hour funds availability.
Ensure that two signing officers, on file with Central 1, have signed the form.
Send the completed form to Central 1 as directed on the form.
Related Information
Central 1 Account Request,
Form
3571
5.3	Posting Incoming AFT Transactions to the Financial Institution芒聙聶s Account at Central 1
Each business day, AFT transactions with that day芒聙聶s due date or earlier are posted as indicated below. Transactions due on a weekend or on a holiday are posted on the following business day.
The following table summarizes when incoming AFT transactions are posted to the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account:
Date Transaction Received by Central聽1
Date Posted to Financial Institution芒聙聶s Central 1 Account
On or before due date
Due date
On or before due date when the due date isn芒聙聶t a business day
Business day after the due date
After due date
Date received or next business day
Canadian Dollar AFT Transactions
The total of all Canadian dollar AFT transactions is posted to the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account by run. Since credits (direct deposits) and debits (pre-authorized debits) are consolidated in a net settlement, there can be up to four postings. If the net amount is balanced, meaning 0, there is no entry posted to the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account. If the net amount isn芒聙聶t balanced, either a credit or debit will be posted. Credits and debits are coded as 芒聙聹AS芒聙聺 (AFT Settlement). See the Account Activity and Balances Report (AIBC for BC/AIOC for ON) and the Daily AFT Clearing Settlement 芒聙聯 AFT Clearing (ICPS).
Note:	AFT credit and debit settlement postings also appear on CAS Online. In addition to the description 芒聙聹AS AFT Settlement芒聙聺, we will reference the clearing file associated with the posting. Financial institutions in Ontario will see the description 芒聙聹AS AFT Settlement芒聙聺 followed by ON01, ON02, ON03, or ON04. Financial institutions in BC will see the description 芒聙聹AS AFT Settlement芒聙聺 followed by CR01, CR02, CR03, or CR04.
Refer to the following reports to view incoming Canadian dollar AFT transactions:
Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD) 芒聙聯 Obtain this report to view the details of the incoming pre-authorized debits and direct deposits by branch.
Summary of Automated Transfer System (CCS) 芒聙聯 Obtain this report to see a summary of incoming AFT totals by financial institution.
Note:	See the AFT Reports Part for more information on these reports.
The transaction data is also forwarded to the financial institution芒聙聶s banking system for posting to the members芒聙聶 accounts. See Section 5.4, Posting Incoming AFT Transactions to Member Accounts 芒聙聯 Automatically via Banking System, and Section 5.5, Posting Incoming AFT Transactions to Member Accounts 芒聙聯 Manually.
US Dollar AFT Transactions
There is one run of incoming US dollar AFT transactions per business day in BC and two runs in Ontario. If the net amount is balanced, meaning 0, there is no entry posted to the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account. If the net amount isn芒聙聶t balanced, either a credit or debit will be posted. Credits and debits are coded as 芒聙聹AS芒聙聺 (AFT Settlement). BC financial institutions refer to the Account Activity and Balances Report (AIBU). Ontario financial institutions refer to the Account Activity and Balances Report (AIOU). For a breakdown of AFT transactions, also refer to either the Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing Report (UCD) or Summary of Automated Transfer System Report (UCS).
When there is a US holiday, settlement for US dollar AFT occurs the next US business day. If there are no US dollar AFT transactions for your financial institution, Central 1 sends a null file to the financial institution banking service provider.
Refer to the following reports to view incoming US dollar AFT transactions:
Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (UCD) 芒聙聯 Obtain this report to view the details of the incoming pre-authorized debits and direct deposits by branch.
Summary of Automated Transfer System (UCS) 芒聙聯 Obtain this report to see a summary of incoming US dollar AFT totals by financial institution.
Note:	See the AFT Reports Part for more information on these reports.
The transaction data is also forwarded to the financial institution芒聙聶s banking system for posting to the members芒聙聶 accounts.
5.4	Posting Incoming AFT Transactions to Member Accounts 芒聙聯 Automatically via Banking System
Most financial institutions have a banking system that automatically posts incoming AFT transactions to the members芒聙聶 accounts on the due date.
Incoming AFT transactions are posted as indicated below. If a system problem prevents automatic posting of incoming direct deposits, see System Problem 芒聙聯 Manually Posting Direct Deposits on Due Date in Section 5.5, Posting Incoming AFT Transactions to Member Accounts 芒聙聯 Manually.
Canadian Dollar AFT Transactions
Central 1 delivers Canadian dollar AFT transaction details, with a due date of today or earlier, to financial institutions with an in-house banking system and to third-party banking system providers four times a day, each calendar day.
BC
芒聙聯 The Canadian dollar AFT clearing files are known as CR01, CR02, CR03, and CR04.
Ontario
芒聙聯 The Canadian dollar AFT clearing files are known as ON01, ON02, ON03, ON04, and ONRT.
Download these files from Central 1芒聙聶s File and Report Exchange in the financial institution芒聙聶s root directory. The financial institution can view the details of these files by obtaining the Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD) report.
Although Canadian dollar files are delivered multiple times each calendar day, contact your banking system provider to determine how frequently the files are posted to the member芒聙聶s accounts. Financial institutions with an in-house banking system can download the above files and determine how frequently the transactions will be posted. See Section 5.5, Posting Incoming AFT Transactions to Member Accounts 芒聙聯 Manually.
The table below describes when Central 1 makes each run of Canadian dollar AFT transactions available:
BC Run
Ontario Run
Guaranteed Time Available
CR01
ON01
8:59 pm PT/11:59 pm ET the day before the due date
CR02
ON02
8:30 am PT/11:30 am ET on due date
CR03
ON03
3:30 pm PT/6:30 pm ET on due date
CR04
ON04
ONRT
8:00 pm PT/11:00 pm ET on due date
US Dollar AFT Transactions
US dollar (USD) AFT transactions are contained in a USD clearing file known as UC01 for BC and UN01 and UNRT for Ontario. The USD clearing files are delivered once a day, each business day, to financial institutions with an in-house banking system and to third-party banking system providers. The files are also delivered on US and Canadian holidays, but settlement does not occur until the next business day. Download these files from Central 1芒聙聶s File and Report Exchange. The UC01, UN01, and UNRT files are available in the financial institution芒聙聶s root directory. The financial institution can view the details of these files by obtaining the UCD report.
The table below describes when Central 1 makes US dollar AFT transactions available to the banking systems.
Guaranteed Time Available
Comment
BC PT
Ontario ET
9:00 am on the due date
12:00 pm on the due date
Monday 芒聙聯 Friday, including Canadian and US statutory holidays
5.5	Posting Incoming AFT Transactions to Member Accounts 芒聙聯 Manually
Incoming AFT transactions are manually posted to the members芒聙聶 financial institution accounts if the following occurs:
A financial institution does not have an in-house banking system or a third-party banking system provider that automatically posts AFT transactions.
The banking system is unable to handle US dollar AFT postings automatically.
The automatic posting process isn芒聙聶t functioning.
Manually Posting Canadian and US Dollar AFT Transactions
Use the Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD/UCD) report to post incoming AFT transactions to the members芒聙聶 accounts. Obtain the CCD reports from the financial institution芒聙聶s CRF* Canadian AFT report file and UCD from the financial institution芒聙聶s MERG file. Download the CRF* Canadian AFT and MERG files from Central 1芒聙聶s File and Report Exchange in the financial institution芒聙聶s root directory. If applicable, the CCD and UCD reports are also available as PDFs in the AFT folder. On the due date, obtain the CCD from the appropriate CRF* file. The day after one or more non- business days or US holidays, obtain the UCD for the previous days. See the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for information on accessing the File and Report Exchange.
Note: 	If the financial institution uses Serviceability Code 芒聙聹0芒聙聺, it is the financial institution芒聙聶s responsibility to ensure that incoming Canadian AFT credit transactions are posted and made available to members芒聙聶 accounts on the due date within two hours of receiving the AFT clearing data from Central 1.
Manually post each transaction to the appropriate account. Your financial institution may have a policy of back-dating items with a due date earlier than the current date. Ensure that direct deposits are posted by the start of business on the due date. Refer to the table below to determine how to post each record type.
Record
Post as a Credit
Post as a Debit
Description
C
X
Credit 芒聙聯 Original direct deposit that is initiated at another financial institution
D
X
Debit 芒聙聯 Original pre-authorized debit that is initiated at another financial institution
E
X
Reversal of an original credit transaction (C record) that is initiated at another financial institution
F
X
Reversal of an original debit transaction (D record) that is initiated at another financial institution
I
X
Return of a credit transaction (C record). The original credit was sent from a financial institution or business member originator to another financial institution. That institution returned it.
Return of a credit error correction (F record). A financial institution or business member originator initiated the error correction but the receiving financial institution returned it. Disputed error corrections are returned via paper voucher.
If applicable, post the offsetting entry for the return of a debit transaction. This applies only to financial institutions that generate returns via the Online Return System (ORS), or via a batch return program that does not automatically create the offsetting entry.
Recall of a credit transaction
J
X
Return of a debit transaction (D record). The original debit was sent from a financial institution or business member originator to another financial institution. That institution returned it.
Return of a debit error correction (E record). A financial institution or business member originator initiated the error correction but the receiving financial institution returned it.
If applicable, post the offsetting entry for the return of a credit transaction. This applies only to financial institutions that generate returns via the Online Return System (ORS), or via a batch return program that does not automatically create the offsetting entry.
Recall of a debit transaction (from a file recall as individual debit transactions can芒聙聶t be recalled).
System Problem 芒聙聯 Manually Posting Direct Deposits on Due Date
CPA Rule F1, Section 8, Credit Transaction Funds Availability, specifies that direct deposits must be available to the payee as follows, based on the financial institution芒聙聶s Serviceability Code:
Serviceability Code 芒聙聹0芒聙聺 芒聙聯 AFT credit transactions must be made available to the member within two hours of receiving the AFT clearing file that contains the AFT credit transaction.
Serviceability Code 芒聙聹1芒聙聺 or 芒聙聹2芒聙聺 芒聙聯 AFT credit transactions must be made available to the member by the start of business on the due date of the transaction.
If an Originating financial institution has submitted a direct deposit within the recommended time frame, but the receiving financial institution encounters a system problem that prevents the automatic posting of the direct deposit transaction, the financial institution must decide whether or not to manually post the transaction. If a financial institution manually posts a transaction and the system problem is resolved, this could result in duplicate postings of the transaction. To avoid duplicate postings, it is recommended that the financial institution develop internal policies on manually posting direct deposits when there is a system problem. Financial institutions can refer to Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD/UCD) reports to obtain details on the direct deposits.
Ensure that these reports are available to all branches by the start of business or ensure that a contact at your head office is available to take branch inquires.
Note:	See the AFT Reports Part for more information on this report.
5.6	Unable to Post AFT Credits within Required Time Frame
If the financial institution is unable to post AFT credits to members芒聙聶 accounts, either automatically or manually, within the required time frame, contact Central 1芒聙聶s Client Support Services immediately to report the issue: 1-888-889-7878, Option 1. Provide the information below, as applicable.
Serviceability Code 芒聙聹0芒聙聺
Advise that the financial institution is unable to post AFT credit transactions to members芒聙聶 accounts within two hours of receiving the AFT clearing file that contained the AFT credit transactions.
Provide a contact name to Central 1 for additional follow up, if necessary.
Provide an estimate of when AFT credits will be posted to members芒聙聶 accounts, if known.
5.7	Unpostable AFT Transactions
It is recommended that all financial institutions, even those with automatically posted transactions, review their AFT processing daily for exception items. Transactions with an incorrect account type or account number can be rerouted to the correct account if the financial institution has the new information and wants to proceed. Alternatively, these transactions can be returned to the originator. If a transaction can芒聙聶t be posted to the member芒聙聶s account, (NSF or account is closed), it must be returned to the originator芒聙聶s financial institution. See the Online Return System Part. BC financial institutions that return AFT transactions via the Batch Return Service or via Central 1 (for US dollar transactions), can see the AFT Returns and AFT Notice of Change Transactions Part.
Also check the Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD/UCD) report daily. Exception items listed on this report include returns (I and J records) and error corrections (E and F records) from other financial institutions. See AFT Rejects, Recalls, Error Corrections, and Other Exception Items Part.
END
